verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast are poor/zero, and wind is above your normal limit.

conversationalSummary.text: TOMORROW at Baleal, it’s probably not worth chasing—there’s no reliable window and the wind is the main problem (cross-on and strong), so even lunch probably won’t feel right. With low confidence on the conditions, I’d treat it as a skip unless you get a pleasant surprise on arrival. If you do go, just keep your plan flexible and be ready to leave quickly if it’s still too windy.

best.windFit: N 30 km/h · cross-onshore · too windy, strong
best.bestWind: ideal E/ESE/SE, usable NE/ENE/SSE, clean <=25 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Low confidence: 0.9-1.2 m, 289 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 72/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; 10-13s at mid tide suits the Baleal sandbanks and gives workable peaks.  Watch: wind is over 30 km/h; wind fit is poor or too strong.
best.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind fit is poor or too strong
